mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.25% signal emergency monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS is at or near zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% on 2020-06-01 indicates zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 net new jobs, signaling labor market weakness that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137,802k on 2020-06-01 reflects post-COVID recovery phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite payroll deceleration, or signal fails to predict negative returns with directional accuracy > 52% in any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 2%, indicating consumer demand collapse that cascades into earnings downgrades and equity selloffs. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns when demand destruction is confirmed.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month percentage change falls below -2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 485,545M on 2020-05-01 reflects pandemic demand shock recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when RSAFS declines > 2%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment move in opposite directions: INDPRO rising while PAYEMS falls signals productivity gains without labor hoarding, indicating efficient cost structure that supports equity valuations and positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change > 1% AND PAYEMS 3-month change < 50,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.46 on 2020-06-01 shows supply-side recovery
regime_hint: post-shock productivity recovery with labor rationalization
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (INDPRO positive × PAYEMS negative) shows no incremental predictive power beyond single-series models (R² improvement < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 1%, creating negative real rates that erode bond returns and force equity risk premium compression. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive equity returns only in late-cycle expansions where growth expectations offset inflation concerns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 257.21 on 2020-06-01 reflects post-pandemic price stability
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy and rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to differentiate returns between late-cycle and early-cycle regimes (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 900,000 annualized units while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive, signaling demand rotation away from housing toward other consumption that sustains equity earnings. This always-on mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns when housing weakness is decoupled from broader growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 974k on 2020-05-01 reflects pandemic-era housing demand normalization
regime_hint: demand rebalancing away from housing in growth-positive environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 48% of observations when HOUST < 900k and GDPC1 growth > 0%, or interaction term adds no predictive value beyond univariate models (marginal R² < 0.005).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration that triggers equity selloffs as investors reprice recession probability. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 5-day forward returns when unemployment acceleration is confirmed.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 11.1% on 2020-06-01 reflects post-COVID labor market shock
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment acceleration > 0.5pp, or signal fails to achieve directional accuracy > 51% in any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 1% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable, indicating demand-driven supply weakness rather than cost-push inflation. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 60-day forward returns in demand-constrained regimes where supply weakness signals insufficient aggregate demand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -1.0% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month change < 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.46 on 2020-06-01 shows supply-side recovery from pandemic trough
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (INDPRO negative × CPIAUCSL stable) shows no incremental predictive power beyond single-series models (R² improvement < 0.01).
